title: Love your experiments
subtitle: Realizzat per la mostra "Prospectives on AI", Basel Academy of Art and Design FHNW
description:
“Love your experiments (as you would an ugly child). Joy is the engine of growth.”
— Bruce Mau, An Incomplete Manifesto for Growth (1998)

Theoretical rigidity and the self-imposed constraints of “conventional” design lead nowhere. Text–image models are tools, like a feather brush or a tin pen nib, that can help us expand our boundaries and explore new perspectives. The designer’s starting point is to define an appropriate meaning with an expressive sign for a given context. Playing, experimenting, daring, and having fun are not about the tools, but above all about the content. There should be no limits to research: “art has no limits because what can be known is limitless.” We cannot remain static, doing only what we already know. We desire a better world, and every tool can play its part. By combining images generated by artificial intelligence with a manual touch, we can express deep emotions.
